Contact.title: Contact
Contact.description: Represents a contact, which can be a client, lead, or partner.

firstName.description: The first name of the contact.

lastName.description: The last name of the contact.

email.description: The email address of the contact.

phone.description: The phone number of the contact.

contactType.description: The type of contact (e.g., client, lead, partner).

leadScore.description: The lead score of the contact.

propertyType.description: The type of property (e.g., house, apartment).

contactId.description: Reference to Contact. (Relationship: Contact 1:N Property). Represents the agent or contact associated with the listing.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task to be completed.

status.description: The status of the task (e.g., open, completed).

contactId.description: Reference to Contact. (Relationship: Contact 1:N Task). Represents contact associated with the task.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a report on sales performance, lead conversion rates, and agent activity.

description.description: A description of the report's content.

dateGenerated.description: The date the report was generated.

metrics.description: Key metrics included in the report (e.g., sales performance, lead conversion rates).

sentDate.description: Date and time the email was sent.

contactId.description: Reference to Contact. (Relationship: Contact 1:N Email). Represents contact associated with the email.

definition.description: Stores contacts owned by a specific user. Uses path-based ownership for security. Authorization is based on the `userId` parameter, ensuring only the user can access their contacts.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the contact.

params.name: contactId
params.description: The unique ID of the contact.

definition.description: Stores property listings owned by a specific user. Uses path-based ownership for security.  Authorization is based on the `userId` parameter, ensuring only the user can access their properties.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the property listing.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique ID of the property listing.

definition.description: Stores tasks owned by a specific user. Uses path-based ownership for security. Authorization is based on the `userId` parameter, ensuring only the user can access their tasks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the task.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique ID of the task.

definition.description: Stores reports owned by a specific user. Uses path-based ownership for security. Authorization is based on the `userId` parameter, ensuring only the user can access their reports.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the report.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique ID of the report.

definition.description: Stores emails owned by a specific user. Uses path-based ownership for security. Authorization is based on the `userId` parameter, ensuring only the user can access their emails.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the email.

params.name: emailId
params.description: The unique ID of the email.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage real estate CRM data, emphasizing security, scalability, and ease of debugging. It follows a hierarchical structure for user-owned data and employs denormalization techniques to ensure authorization independence. The structure also segregates data based on access needs, facilitating secure list operations (QAPs) and simplifying security rules. Path-based ownership is prioritized, and data clarity is maintained through explicit state modeling and consistent naming conventions.

Authorization Independence:
Denormalization is implemented to avoid using `get()` calls in security rules. This is achieved by including relevant authorization data (e.g., user roles or membership) directly within the documents needing protection, eliminating hierarchical authorization dependencies.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters):
The structure enables secure `list` operations by segregating data based on access needs. Private user data is stored under the `/users/{userId}` path, ensuring that listing operations can be secured using path-based rules. Shared or collaborative data can be secured via membership maps denormalized into the documents.
